title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality… · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Compose Integration Faults
summary: A release builds successfully but fails at runtime on a clean host, ships a root default, and loses data when its dependency stalls. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose integration faults increment in container release test and fault matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, DNS failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated Compose project.
Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Compose Integration Faults · Theory Docker Compose Integration Faults
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, DNS failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated Compose project.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Compose Integration Faults contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Compose Integration Faults · Theory Docker Compose Integration Faults.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, dns failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated compose project. For Predict Docker Compose Integration Faults · Theory Docker Compose Integration Faults, keep Docker Compose Integration Faults connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, DNS failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated Compose project. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Integration Faults, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container release test and fault matrix.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-2
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: worked docker testing validation quality 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Starting all services once is adequate multi-container integration evidence. This fails the Docker Compose Integration Faults contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
